secAlertTitle: セキュリティアラート
secAlertSsl: この接続は安全ではありません。TLSを有効にしてデータ保護を行うまで、機密情報を入力しないでください。
secAlertConf: 一部の設定は脆弱です。潜在的な脆弱性を防ぐために、セキュリティプロトコルを強化することをお勧めします。
secAlertSSL: セキュアな接続がありません。データ保護のためにTLS証明書をインストールしてください。
secAlertPanelPort: デフォルトのポートにはセキュリティリスクがあります。ランダムなポートまたは特定のポートを設定してください。
secAlertPanelURI: デフォルトのURIパスは安全ではありません。複雑なURIパスを設定してください。
secAlertSubURI: サブスクリプションのデフォルトURIパスは安全ではありません。複雑なURIパスを設定してください。
secAlertSubJsonURI: JSONサブスクリプションのデフォルトURIパスは安全ではありません。複雑なURIパスを設定してください。
emptyDnsDesc: 追加されたDNSサーバーはありません。
emptyFakeDnsDesc: 追加されたFake DNSサーバーはありません。
emptyBalancersDesc: 追加されたバランサーはありません。
emptyReverseDesc: 追加されたリバースプロキシはありません。
somethingWentWrong: エラーが発生しました

pages.settings.subOnlyHappV2RayTunDesc: サブスクリプションをフィルタリングして、HappおよびV2RayTunと互換性のあるリンク（vless、vmess、trojan、shadowsocks）のみを含める
pages.settings.subHideConfigLinks: サブスクリプションページで設定リンクを非表示にする

pages.xray.title: Xray 設定
pages.xray.restartSuccess: Xrayの再起動に成功しました
pages.xray.stopSuccess: Xrayが正常に停止しました
pages.xray.restartError: Xrayの再起動中にエラーが発生しました。
pages.xray.stopError: Xrayの停止中にエラーが発生しました。
pages.xray.generalConfigsDesc: これらのオプションは一般設定を決定します
pages.xray.logConfigsDesc: ログはサーバーのパフォーマンスに影響を与える可能性があるため、必要な場合にのみ有効にすることをお勧めします
pages.xray.blockConfigsDesc: これらのオプションは、特定のプロトコルやウェブサイトへのユーザー接続をブロックします
pages.xray.blockConnectionsConfigsDesc: これらのオプションにより、特定のリクエスト元の国に基づいてトラフィックをブロックします。
pages.xray.directConnectionsConfigsDesc: 直接接続により、特定のトラフィックが他のサーバーを経由しないようにします。
pages.xray.ipv4RoutingDesc: このオプションはIPv4のみを介してターゲットドメインへルーティングします
pages.xray.warpRoutingDesc: 注意：これらのオプションを使用する前に、パネルのGitHubの手順に従って、サーバーにsocks5プロキシモードでWARPをインストールしてください。WARPはCloudflareサーバー経由でトラフィックをウェブサイトにルーティングします。
pages.xray.Template: 高度なXray設定テンプレート
pages.xray.TemplateDesc: 最終的なXray設定ファイルはこのテンプレートに基づいて生成されます
pages.xray.FreedomStrategyDesc: Freedomプロトコル内のネットワークの出力戦略を設定する

pages.xray.Torrent: BitTorrent プロトコルをブロック

pages.xray.dnsLogDesc: DNSクエリのログを有効にするかどうか

hwidBetaWarningDesc: HWID tracking is currently in beta version and works only with happ and v2raytun clients. Other clients may not support HWID registration.
pages.login.toasts.needTwoFactor: Enter the authentication code, or submit again to receive it via Telegram if enabled.
pages.login.toasts.twoFactorTelegramSent: A login code was sent to your Telegram admin chat(s).
pages.index.usersOnlineHint: Distinct client emails with active usage or presence (same source as the client list / traffic collection).
pages.inbounds.editInboundHint: Protocol cannot be changed for an existing inbound.
pages.inbounds.hysteriaAuthDesc: Hysteria2 shared secret (mapped to auth in Xray).
pages.inbounds.addInboundEditorFollowUp: A full in-panel editor (protocol, stream, security, clients) is planned in a follow-up. This view lists inbounds and supports delete only.
pages.inbounds.addInboundApiBlurb: Create a new inbound with a POST and JSON body (protocol, port, tag, streamSettings, sniffing, and related fields — see the reference).
pages.inbounds.filterNoResults: No inbounds match the current filters.
pages.inbounds.addInboundModalStreamNote: Transport: TCP, security: none. For Reality, WebSocket, or custom TLS, use POST /panel/api/inbounds/add with a full body (see API docs).
pages.inbounds.addInboundFirstClientHint: Shown in the panel; must be unique among clients. Used as subscription id in many clients.
pages.inbounds.addInboundNodesHint: Only in multi-node mode. Leave all unchecked to assign later.
pages.inbounds.wireguardSettingsHint: Server-side WireGuard (UDP): MTU, private key, tunnel addresses, optional peers (client public keys), noKernelTun, optional workers. Settings JSON is built on the server. There are no panel-managed client entries for this protocol.
pages.inbounds.wireguardPeersEmpty: No peers yet. Add a row for each client public key (optional PSK, allowedIPs).
pages.inbounds.wireguardTransportHint: WireGuard uses UDP on the inbound port. There is no separate TCP/WebSocket `streamSettings` — leave the generated empty `{}` and set MTU, keys, addresses, and peers in Protocol authentication on the next step.
pages.settings.dockerUpdaterTriggerSucce: Update job was sent to the Docker sidecar. The panel may restart in a moment.
pages.settings.dockerUpdaterTriggerError: Could not trigger the Docker sidecar updater.
pages.settings.remarkModelDesc: The first character is the separator in subscription display names. The rest are a sequence of: i = inbound name, e = user email, o = per-client / extra remark, n = node name, p = node host, r = port.
pages.settings.remarkModelOrder: Field order (letters i e o n p r)
pages.settings.remarkModelOrderHint: Reorder with the arrows; add segments with +i … +r. Default -ieo joins inbound, email, and extra with the separator. Invalid letters are ignored when saving.
pages.settings.remarkModelOrderEmpty: No fields in the sequence yet — add letters below.
pages.settings.tgRunTimeOptionDailyAt: Every day at a set time
pages.settings.tgRunTimeCustomHint: Six fields: second, minute, hour, day, month, weekday. Example: 0 0 8 * * * is every day at 08:00.
pages.settings.subShowOnlyHappV2RayTunDe: Display only Happ and V2RayTun application buttons on the subscription page, hiding all others
pages.settings.subProviderIDDesc: Provider identifier for using Happ extended headers (new-url, new-domain, etc.). Usually a UUID. Required for Happ extended features to work.
pages.settings.subProviderIDMethodDesc: Choose how to send Provider ID to clients according to Happ documentation: in URL as query parameter (?providerid=...), in HTTP response header (providerid: ...), in subscription body as comment (#providerid ...), or disabled
pages.settings.multiNodeModeDesc: Enable distributed architecture with separate worker nodes. When enabled, XRAY Core runs on nodes instead of locally.
pages.settings.multiNodeModePoint1: XRAY Core will not run locally
pages.settings.multiNodeModePoint2: Configurations will be sent to worker nodes
pages.settings.multiNodeModePoint3: You need to assign inbounds to nodes
pages.settings.enableMultiNodeModeConfir: Enabling multi-node mode will stop local XRAY Core. Make sure you have configured worker nodes before enabling this mode. Continue?
pages.settings.languageDesc: Applies to this browser only. The choice is saved in local storage on your device.
pages.settings.panelThemeDesc: Choose accent palette and background. The choice is saved in this browser only.
pages.settings.grafanaEnableDesc: When enabled, all logs (including xray and node logs) will be sent to Grafana Loki, and local file logging will be disabled. All log levels will be enabled.
pages.settings.grafanaDashboardDesc: Download ready-to-use Grafana dashboard JSON file
pages.settings.grafanaMetricsEndpointDes:
Metrics are available at: /panel/metrics

To configure Grafana/Prometheus scraping:
1. Add a Prometheus data source in Grafana pointing to your SharX panel
2. Configure scrape job with target: http://your-panel-host:port/panel/metrics
3. Import the downloaded dashboard JSON file

Example Prometheus scrape config:
- job_name: 'sharx'
  scrape_interval: 30s
  static_configs:
    - targets: ['your-panel-host:port']
      metrics_path: '/panel/metrics'
pages.settings.grafanaIntegrationEnabled:
When Grafana integration is enabled:
• All logs (x-ui, xray, and node logs) will be sent to Loki
• Local file logging will be disabled
• All log levels (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR) will be enabled
• Metrics will be exported in Prometheus format
• Metrics can be scraped from /panel/metrics endpoint
pages.settings.sections.subAdvancedJsonH: Large JSON snippets for clients. Expand only when you need them.
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.title: Subscription Headers
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.profi: Subscription name shown in the application (max 25 chars for Happ)
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.subsc: Traffic and expiry information: upload=X; download=Y; total=Z; expire=T
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.suppo: Support page link (displayed as icon on the right)
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.profi: Subscription website link (displayed as icon on the left)
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.annou: Announcement text for users (max 200 chars, supports base64)
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.newDo: New domain for subscription without changing full URL (requires Provider ID)
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.serve: Additional server description (max 30 chars, supports base64)
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.subEx: Link for 'Renew' button in expiry notification
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.subIn: Button text in information block (max 25 chars)
Link opened when clicking button in information block
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.updat: Force subscription update on every app open (true)
pages.settings.migration.title: Migration
pages.settings.migration.infoDesc: Migrate data from old SQLite database (x-ui.db) to new PostgreSQL database. Clients will be extracted from JSON fields and converted to new structure.
pages.settings.migration.preview.panelSe: The following panel settings from the old database will be applied after migration. Remember them, as the container will be restarted with new settings.
pages.settings.migration.panelRestartWar: Warning: After successful migration, the Docker container will be automatically restarted to apply new settings. Please remember the new settings (port, path, etc.) as you may need them to reconnect.
pages.settings.migration.result.panelRes: Container has been restarted with the new settings. If you cannot access the panel, check the new port and path settings.
pages.settings.migration.error.invalidFi: Invalid file type. Please select a .db file
This will migrate data from SQLite to PostgreSQL. Existing data may be updated. Continue?
pages.settings.security.twoFactorBeginUn: You must be logged in to set up two-factor authentication.
pages.settings.security.twoFactorBeginEr: Could not start authenticator setup. Try again.
pages.settings.security.twoFactorComplet: Could not complete authenticator setup. Start over from the QR step.
pages.settings.security.twoFactorTelegra: After a correct password, sends the same 6-digit code to admin chat IDs (Telegram settings) so you can sign in without an authenticator app. Requires the bot enabled and running.
pages.settings.security.twoFactorDisable: Disable two-factor authentication? You can set it up again afterward.
pages.settings.security.apiTokenHint: Use Bearer tokens for scripts and API clients without browser cookies. Store them safely; the full value is shown only once when created.
pages.settings.security.apiTokenConfirmR: Revoke this token? Scripts and clients using it will stop working.
pages.settings.security.apiTokenModalHin: Copy it now. You will not be able to see the full token again.
pages.settings.subCustomize.themeDesc: Select a visual theme for the subscription page
pages.settings.subCustomize.logoUrlDesc: URL of the logo image (32x32 or 64x64 pixels)
pages.settings.subCustomize.brandTextDes: Text to display next to the logo
pages.xray.nodeModeInfo: In node mode, core settings are applied through configuration profiles. Save settings as a profile to apply them to nodes.
pages.xray.TemplateDescStandalone: Edit the configuration JSON and use Save & apply — settings go to the local Xray core. Stand-alone mode has no separate profile to publish to nodes.
pages.xray.runtimeViewStandalone: Read-only: JSON currently loaded by the core on this server.
pages.xray.sliceEditorHintStandalone: Edit this section as JSON or use the steps above. When you save, the template is applied to the local core.
pages.xray.runtimeView: Read-only: JSON currently applied. In node mode, edit the template or profiles, then apply to nodes as usual.
pages.xray.sliceEditorHint: Edit this section as JSON, or use the top steps for a guided layout.
pages.xray.stepDesc.general: Quick options and log paths without editing full JSON
pages.xray.stepDesc.additionalTopLevel: Additional top-level keys in this template
pages.xray.outbound.wireguardHint: Edit WireGuard settings as JSON (peers, keys, MTU).
pages.xray.outbound.genericProtocolHint: Protocol “{{protocol}}”: adjust settings JSON; add streamSettings if needed.
pages.xray.outbound.streamSettingsJsonHi: tcpSettings, wsSettings, grpcSettings, and other fields. Use when something is not covered by the fields above.
pages.xray.dnsBuilder.enableCustom: Use custom DNS object in template
pages.xray.dnsBuilder.hostsHint: Hosts map (JSON object: hostname → IP or array of IPs)
pages.xray.dnsBuilder.disabledHint: DNS is null; the node uses system/default resolvers unless set elsewhere.
pages.xray.routingBuilder.advancedOnly: This routing uses balancers or rules the visual editor cannot represent. Edit as JSON, or remove balancers/complex rules to use the simple rule list.
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.title: Core Config Profiles
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.deleteProfi: Are you sure you want to delete profile
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.name: Name

pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.resetToDefa: Are you sure you want to reset profile
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.coreConfigP: Select Xray core configuration profile for this outbound. If not selected, default profile will be used.
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.noProfilesA: No profiles available. Create a profile on the 'Core Config Profiles' page.
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.assignNodes: Select nodes to assign to this profile. Each node can only be assigned to one profile.
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.pageDesc: Templates for Xray on nodes. Assign a profile to each node; edit by section or General.
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.multiNodeOf: Enable multi-node mode in panel settings to use core config profiles.
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.addHint: Config is copied from the default profile (or the first one). Edit it after creation.
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.deleteProfi: This cannot be undone. Profiles used by outbounds cannot be deleted.
pages.nodes.title: Nodes Management
pages.nodes.name: Name
pages.nodes.saveSecretKeyHint: Set on the worker as SECRET_KEY or SHARX_NODE_SECRET_KEY. Shown only once — store it safely.
pages.nodes.dockerComposeHint: Ready-to-paste docker-compose (PANEL_URL is this panel; change the image if you use another registry).
pages.nodes.legacyAuth: Legacy registration (API key only, no SECRET_KEY)
pages.nodes.saveApiKeyHint: Save this API key on the node (NODE_API_KEY). It will not be shown again in full.
pages.nodes.validUrl: Must be a valid URL (http:// or https://)
pages.nodes.validPort: Port must be a number between 1 and 65535
pages.nodes.duplicateNode: A node with this address and port already exists
pages.nodes.fullUrlHint: Pairing: in the database you can store http://; the panel still uses TLS to the node API. Without a scheme, https:// is used. Legacy without TLS: enable HTTP in the block below.
pages.nodes.composeFirstStepDesc: The panel uses a single shared SECRET_KEY, so this compose file works for every node you add. Deploy the container first, then come back and click Next.
pages.nodes.copyComposeOnlyHint: The compose snippet already contains PANEL_URL and the panel-wide SECRET_KEY. Paste it into docker-compose.yml on the node host.
pages.nodes.registerInProgress: Please wait — connecting to the node.
pages.nodes.registerFlowHint: The node will be created in the panel, then the panel will verify it can reach the node API.
pages.nodes.registerStepDeployHint: SECRET_KEY (or API key in legacy mode) is ready as soon as the node record exists — you can start the container while the check below runs, or after a failed check use Retry when the node is up.
pages.nodes.draftPendingHint: A node was created but the health check did not pass. You can try again, or remove the draft to start over. Editing the name or address clears the draft for a new run.
pages.nodes.copyError: Could not copy. Try again or select and copy the text manually from the browser.
pages.nodes.hostUrlNoPortHint: Typical public URL (port omitted; worker listens on 8080 on the host): {{url}}
pages.nodes.authPairingDescription: The worker is autonomous: run it with SECRET_KEY on the host. The panel uses mTLS and JWT. No /register to the panel.
pages.nodes.editPairingNote: Pairing material is not edited here. Change the address or limit, or remove and re-add the node to rotate keys.
pages.nodes.apiKeyHint: API key configured on the node (NODE_API_KEY environment variable)
pages.nodes.deleteConfirmText: Are you sure you want to delete this node?
pages.nodes.useTlsHint: Enable TLS/HTTPS for API calls to this node
pages.nodes.certPathHint: Path to CA certificate file (optional, for custom CA)
pages.nodes.keyPathHint: Path to private key file (optional, for client certificate)
pages.nodes.insecureTlsHint: ⚠️ Not recommended: Skip TLS certificate verification (insecure)
pages.nodes.trafficLimitGBHint: Set traffic limit in GB (0 = unlimited). When limit is reached, node will be blocked.
pages.nodes.resetTrafficConfirm: Are you sure you want to reset traffic statistics for this node?
pages.nodes.statsHint: Values are updated by the panel on a schedule (the same per-node database counters as elsewhere).
pages.nodes.geography.title: Node geography
pages.nodes.geography.hint: Approximate locations from public IP (panel on startup; each node when the worker starts). Lines connect nodes to the panel.
pages.nodes.geography.legendHint: Blue: panel. Green/gray: node (green when online). Lines: link to the panel. Nearby points are grouped; the badge shows how many.
pages.nodes.geography.panelPending: Panel location not available yet. It is filled after the next successful lookup on panel startup (outbound HTTPS required).
pages.nodes.geography.panZoomHint: Scroll or pinch to zoom; drag to pan. Zoom in close to split a group into separate pins.
pages.nodes.registerStep.create.title: Register in the panel
pages.nodes.registerStep.create.desc: Create the node record and pairing (SECRET_KEY) in the database.
pages.nodes.registerStep.verify.title: Connect to the node
pages.nodes.registerStep.verify.desc: Run a health check from the panel to the node API.
pages.nodes.toasts.assignError: Failed to assign inbound to node

pages.clients.addModalTgIdHint: 0 or empty = not linked to Telegram
pages.clients.addModalAnnounceHint: Optional. Max 200 characters, displayed in the subscription.
pages.clients.addModalAdvancedProtocolHi: Empty fields use autogenerated UUID, subscription ID, and protocol defaults for chosen inbounds.
pages.clients.addModalCommentTooLong: Comment must be at most 100 characters
pages.clients.addModalAnnounceTooLong: Announcement must be at most 200 characters
pages.clients.subIdInFormHint: Sub ID can be changed in the subscription field below.
pages.clients.customUuidHint: RFC 4122 format. Leave empty to auto-generate on the server.
pages.clients.createdWithUuidCopied: Client created. UUID copied to clipboard.
pages.clients.maxHwidDesc: Set 0 for unlimited devices. If a new device connects and the limit is reached, the connection will be blocked.
pages.clients.registeredHwidsDesc: List of hardware IDs (devices) that have connected to this client. Only active devices count towards the limit.
pages.clients.confirmDeleteHwid: Are you sure you want to delete this device? This will allow a new device to connect if the limit is not reached.
pages.clients.clearHwidContent: Are you sure you want to clear all HWIDs for
pages.clients.clearAllHwidsContent: Are you sure you want to clear all HWIDs for all clients?
pages.clients.setHwidLimitAllContent: Enter the maximum number of devices allowed for all clients:

pages.clients.cardHwidTooltip: HWID restriction is enabled for this client
pages.clients.keysEmptyNoInbounds: This client has no inbounds assigned. Assign inbounds when editing the client, then open Keys again.
pages.clients.keysEmptyCannotBuild: No share links could be built for the assigned inbounds (unsupported protocol, missing password or UUID for this protocol, or panel host/domain settings). Check the inbound type and Settings → subscription domain if links use the panel host.
pages.clients.uuidAfterCreate: Assigned by the server after the client is created.
pages.clients.statsHint: Live snapshot from each worker’s /stats (reset=false). Upload/Download are for the current Xray stat window on that node. “Online” uses the worker’s online client list.
pages.clients.statsEmptyNodes: No worker nodes with inbounds — add nodes or assign inbounds.
pages.clients.statsRowChartHint: Click an email to open a per-user traffic chart by node.

menu.updateModalIntro: SharX runs in Docker: the container does not replace its own image. On the host, pull the new image and recreate the stack — or use the sidecar button below if your compose stack includes Watchtower. We compare this build to the latest GitHub release when the API is reachable.
menu.versionClickHint: Version and updates — click to open
menu.updateDockerHint: Typical flow (adjust compose file path and service name):
menu.updateDockerPull: Pull the new image and recreate containers
menu.updateAfterPull: After the new image is running, this version label in the header will match the release.
menu.updateSidecarDesc: The stack includes Watchtower on the internal Docker network only (no public port). This asks it to pull the latest image and recreate the panel container.
tutorial.title: Web Panel Menu Guide
tutorial.dashboardDesc:
Main interface for managing the entire system. Through the panel we:

• Configure and control nodes (servers with xray)
• Manage clients
• Configure inbounds
tutorial.dashboardHint: Panel is the control center. Here you create nodes, inbounds and assign clients.
tutorial.nodeDesc: Separate Xray core with API for communication with the panel. The node handles connections and serves as the 'brain' for inbounds.
tutorial.nodeHint: Node is the server core. The panel communicates with it via API to manage configs and connections.
tutorial.inboundDesc:
Configuration or profile for a node.

• Creates a connection to a node
• Subscribes to one or more nodes
tutorial.inboundHint: Inbound is a connection profile. Through it, clients get access to the required nodes.
tutorial.clientDesc:
System user who can be assigned one or more inbounds.

For example:
• Inbound 1 → whitelist node
• Inbound 2 → regular foreign server

Client can use any or all inbounds assigned to them
tutorial.clientHint: Client is a user. Assign inbounds to them so they can connect to the required nodes.
tutorial.hostDesc:
External addresses for connection.

• Proxy balancer that hides direct node addresses
• Can distribute load between multiple nodes
• Replace node address with the required host in inbound

Example:
Inbound connects to a balancer host, which then distributes the connection to real nodes
tutorial.hostHint: Hosts are virtual addresses. Use them for load balancing and hiding real servers.
tutorial.settingsDesc:
Section for general panel configuration.

• Enable/disable various features
• Configure panel appearance and behavior
tutorial.settingsHint: Panel Settings — here you can manage features and panel configuration.
tutorial.xrayDesc:
Section for fine-tuning Xray core.

• Routing
• Connection parameters and traffic routing
• Additional advanced node configs
tutorial.xrayHint: Xray Configuration — for advanced core configuration, routing management and other node parameters.
subBuilder.clientRouting.title: Client routing profiles
subBuilder.clientRouting.intro: Edit routing JSON once, then export deep links for Happ, Incy, SharX, or any app that uses the same …://routing/add/{payload} pattern. Stored in routing.profiles.
subBuilder.clientRouting.deepLinkSchemeH: Used when copying the import link. JSON payload is the same; only the URL scheme changes.
subBuilder.clientRouting.customPrefixHin: Include the path up to and including routing/add/ — the panel appends Base64 JSON.
subBuilder.clientRouting.importHint: Paste a deep link (happ://, incy://, sharx://, …) with /routing/add/, or Base64 / JSON.
subBuilder.clientRouting.importInvalid: Could not decode. Paste a deep link (…://routing/add/…), Base64, or JSON.
subBuilder.clientRouting.dnsHostsHint: One mapping per line: hostname tab IP (or hostname space IP).
subBuilder.clientRouting.emptyDesc: Add a profile to author DIRECT / PROXY / BLOCK lists and copy deep links (happ://, incy://, sharx://, …) for clients.
subBuilder.migrated.title: Config was upgraded to sharx-v2
subBuilder.migrated.text: Your existing branding is preserved. Default blocks were added.
subBuilder.raw.hint: Sharx-v1 and sharx-v2 are both accepted; v1 is migrated to v2 automatically.
subBuilder.empty.title: No blocks yet
subBuilder.empty.text: Pick a block from the palette to start building the page.
subBuilder.preview.label: Live preview
subBuilder.palette.title: Add a block
subBuilder.branding.title: Page title
subBuilder.branding.logoUrlHint: Leave empty to use the built-in icon.
subBuilder.branding.supportUrlHint: Telegram, Discord, VK or a generic link.
subBuilder.branding.colorPresetHint: Same presets as the panel appearance. Applies to dark style; light mode keeps the standard light shell.
subBuilder.responseRules.profileTitleHin: Sent as Profile-Title (base64). Happ allows up to 25 chars.
subBuilder.responseRules.announceHint: Shown in-app (max 200 chars). Clients can override per-user.
subBuilder.responseRules.profileWebPageU: Profile-Web-Page-Url header, shown as a link in some clients.
subBuilder.responseRules.extraHeadersHin: Any additional HTTP headers sent with the subscription response (e.g. Sub-Info-Color).
subBuilder.appSettings.encrypt.title: Encrypt base subscription
subBuilder.appSettings.encrypt.desc: Require Happ/v2raytun/browser; everything else is blocked from the base endpoint.
subBuilder.appSettings.showInfo.title: Include traffic/expiry in remark
subBuilder.appSettings.showInfo.desc: Append remaining traffic and expiry to each config name.
subBuilder.jsonTemplates.fragmentHint: Stream-settings fragmenter (xray). Left empty = no fragmentation injected into JSON sub.
subBuilder.jsonTemplates.muxHint: Per-outbound Mux settings merged into the JSON subscription.
subBuilder.jsonTemplates.noisesHint: Array of noise settings injected alongside fragments.
subBuilder.jsonTemplates.rulesHint: Extra routing rules prepended to the routing section of JSON sub.
subBuilder.blocks.kinds.subscriptionInfo: User overview, status, traffic and expiration in one section.
subBuilder.blocks.kinds.metrics.label: Metrics
subBuilder.blocks.kinds.metrics.summary: Compact grid with the most important stats.
subBuilder.blocks.kinds.installationGuid: Step-by-step how-to for each platform you support.
subBuilder.blocks.kinds.linksList.label: Connection links
subBuilder.blocks.kinds.linksList.summar: Per-config links with copy and QR actions.
subBuilder.blocks.kinds.addToApp.label: Add to app
subBuilder.blocks.kinds.addToApp.summary: One-tap deep-link buttons for Happ / v2rayTun / Hiddify / Streisand / Clash / Karing.
subBuilder.blocks.kinds.supportCta.label: Support CTA
subBuilder.blocks.kinds.supportCta.summa: Highlight a button that links to your support channel.
subBuilder.blocks.kinds.customHtml.label: Custom HTML
subBuilder.blocks.kinds.customHtml.summa: Drop in your own HTML/markup — sanitized before render.
subBuilder.supportCta.title: Heading
subBuilder.customHtml.warningText: Scripts, inline event handlers and javascript: URLs are stripped before rendering.
subBuilder.addToApp.preferJsonUrl: Prefer JSON subscription URL when available
subBuilder.addToApp.label: Label
subBuilder.addToApp.useEncryptedDesc: Generate happ://crypt4 / v2raytun://crypt via the server when available.
subBuilder.install.showDeeplinksDesc: Renders a per-app deep-link button that imports the subscription directly.
subBuilder.install.introPlaceholder: e.g. Install from the App Store, paste the subscription link, connect.
subBuilder.install.label: Label
subBuilder.install.stepText: What the user needs to do
subBuilder.install.noStepsHelp: Leave empty to use the default Install → Import → Connect flow.
